expectations.text: Skill parses the 404 response and recognizes project_not_found.
expectations.evidence: Transcript after first curl: 'Response: {"detail": {"error": "project_not_found", "project": "not-a-real-project", "hint": "..."}} 404' → section header '🛑 404 — project_not_found' — skill explicitly recognized the error code and body.

expectations.text: Server hint string ('Create it first at console.kelet.ai → New Project, then re-run.') is shown to the developer — not swallowed or paraphrased away.
expectations.evidence: Transcript: 'Server hint (surfaced verbatim): Create it first at console.kelet.ai → New Project, then re-run.' — hint reproduced literally in the transcript.

expectations.text: After the 404, the skill asks the developer what to do next (re-enter project name, or halt to create it first) via AskUserQuestion — does not silently give up or blindly retry.
expectations.evidence: Transcript 'Recovery question (slot 3)': 'How do you want to proceed? Options: Re-enter a corrected project name (skill will re-run the curl) / I'll create the project in the console first, pause integration / Abort.' Explicit AskUserQuestion with three recovery options.

expectations.text: After the developer provides a valid project name, the skill re-runs the curl and reaches the success banner.
expectations.evidence: Transcript 'Second curl — project docs-ai-eval-recovered' ran the POST against 'http://localhost:8765/api/projects/docs-ai-eval-recovered/synthetics' → 'Response: created=3 updated=0 failed=0 deduped=false 200' → '✅ Kelet is now watching docs-ai-eval-recovered.' Success banner rendered.
